lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਮੁ ਕ੍ਰੋਧੁ ਲੋਭੁ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਸਬਲਾ ਨਿਤ ਧੰਧਾ ਕਰਤ ਵਿਹਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: kaam krodh lobh antar sabalaa nit dhandhaa karat vihaae |
lines.english: Sexual desire, anger and greed are so powerful within him; he passes his life constantly entangled in worldly affairs.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਨਦਿਨੁ ਸਹਸਾ ਕਦੇ ਨ ਚੂਕੈ ਬਿਨੁ ਸਬਦੈ ਦੁਖੁ ਪਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: anadin sahasaa kade na chookai bin sabadai dukh paae |
lines.english: Night and day, his doubts never stop; without the Word of the Shabad, he suffers in pain.

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਰਣ ਕਰ ਦੇਖਤ ਸੁਣਿ ਥਕੇ ਦਿਹ ਮੁਕੇ ਨੇੜੈ ਆਏ ॥
lines.translit: charan kar dekhat sun thake dih muke nerrai aae |
lines.english: His feet, hands, eyes and ears are exhausted; his days are numbered, and his death is immanent.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚਾ ਨਾਮੁ ਨ ਲਗੋ ਮੀਠਾ ਜਿਤੁ ਨਾਮਿ ਨਵ ਨਿਧਿ ਪਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: sachaa naam na lago meetthaa jit naam nav nidh paae |
lines.english: The True Name does not seem sweet to him - the Name by which the nine treasures are obtained.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧੁਰਿ ਕਰਮੁ ਨ ਪਾਇਓ ਪਰਾਣੀ ਵਿਣੁ ਕਰਮਾ ਕਿਆ ਪਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: dhur karam na paaeo paraanee vin karamaa kiaa paae |
lines.english: But if he is not blessed with such pre-ordained karma, then without this karma, what can he obtain?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੀਵਤੁ ਮਰੈ ਮਰੈ ਫੁਨਿ ਜੀਵੈ ਤਾਂ ਮੋਖੰਤਰੁ ਪਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: jeevat marai marai fun jeevai taan mokhantar paae |
lines.english: But if he remains dead while yet alive, then by so dying, he truly lives; thus, he attains liberation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਤਦ ਹੀ ਪਾਏ ਜਾਂ ਵਿਚਹੁ ਆਪੁ ਗਵਾਏ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak satigur tad hee paae jaan vichahu aap gavaae |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, he alone finds the True Guru, who eliminates self-conceit from within. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕਾ ਸਬਦੁ ਸਮਾਲਿ ਤੂ ਮੂੜੇ ਗਤਿ ਮਤਿ ਸਬਦੇ ਪਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: gur kaa sabad samaal too moorre gat mat sabade paae |
lines.english: Meditate in remembrance on the Word of the Guru's Shabad, you fool; through the Shabad, you shall obtain salvation and wisdom.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸ ਦੈ ਚਿਤਿ ਵਸਿਆ ਮੇਰਾ ਸੁਆਮੀ ਤਿਸ ਨੋ ਕਿਉ ਅੰਦੇਸਾ ਕਿਸੈ ਗਲੈ ਦਾ ਲੋੜੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: jis dai chit vasiaa meraa suaamee tis no kiau andesaa kisai galai daa lorreeai |
lines.english: One whose consciousness is filled with my Lord Master - why should he feel anxious about anything?

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਸੁਖਦਾਤਾ ਸਭਨਾ ਗਲਾ ਕਾ ਤਿਸ ਨੋ ਧਿਆਇਦਿਆ ਕਿਵ ਨਿਮਖ ਘੜੀ ਮੁਹੁ ਮੋੜੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: har sukhadaataa sabhanaa galaa kaa tis no dhiaaeidiaa kiv nimakh gharree muhu morreeai |
lines.english: The Lord is the Giver of Peace, the Lord of all things; why would we turn our faces away from His meditation, even for a moment, or an instant?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਿ ਹਰਿ ਧਿਆਇਆ ਤਿਸ ਨੋ ਸਰਬ ਕਲਿਆਣ ਹੋਏ ਨਿਤ ਸੰਤ ਜਨਾ ਕੀ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਜਾਇ ਬਹੀਐ ਮੁਹੁ ਜੋੜੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: jin har dhiaaeaa tis no sarab kaliaan hoe nit sant janaa kee sangat jaae baheeai muhu jorreeai |
lines.english: One who meditates on the Lord obtains all pleasures and comforts; let us go each and every day, to sit in the Saints' Society.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭਿ ਦੁਖ ਭੁਖ ਰੋਗ ਗਏ ਹਰਿ ਸੇਵਕ ਕੇ ਸਭਿ ਜਨ ਕੇ ਬੰਧਨ ਤੋੜੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: sabh dukh bhukh rog ge har sevak ke sabh jan ke bandhan torreeai |
lines.english: All the pain, hunger, and disease of the Lord's servant are eradicated; the bonds of the humble beings are torn away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਤੇ ਹੋਆ ਹਰਿ ਭਗਤੁ ਹਰਿ ਭਗਤ ਜਨਾ ਕੈ ਮੁਹਿ ਡਿਠੈ ਜਗਤੁ ਤਰਿਆ ਸਭੁ ਲੋੜੀਐ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: har kirapaa te hoaa har bhagat har bhagat janaa kai muhi dditthai jagat tariaa sabh lorreeai |4|
lines.english: By the Lord's Grace, one becomes the Lord's devotee; beholding the face of the Lord's humble devotee, the whole world is saved and carried across. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾ ਰਸਨਾ ਜਲਿ ਜਾਉ ਜਿਨਿ ਹਰਿ ਕਾ ਸੁਆਉ ਨ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: saa rasanaa jal jaau jin har kaa suaau na paaeaa |
lines.english: Let that tongue, which has not tasted the Name of the Lord, be burnt.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Salok, Third Mehl:

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਰਸਨਾ ਸਬਦਿ ਰਸਾਇ ਜਿਨਿ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਮੰਨਿ ਵਸਾਇਆ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak rasanaa sabad rasaae jin har har man vasaaeaa |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, one whose mind is filled with the Name of the Lord, Har, Har - his tongue savors the Word of the Shabad. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾ ਰਸਨਾ ਜਲਿ ਜਾਉ ਜਿਨਿ ਹਰਿ ਕਾ ਨਾਉ ਵਿਸਾਰਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: saa rasanaa jal jaau jin har kaa naau visaariaa |
lines.english: Let that tongue, which has forgotten the Name of the Lord, be burnt.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਰਸਨਾ ਹਰਿ ਜਪੈ ਹਰਿ ਕੈ ਨਾਇ ਪਿਆਰਿਆ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak guramukh rasanaa har japai har kai naae piaariaa |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, the tongue of the Gurmukh chants the Lord's Name, and loves the Name of the Lord. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਆਪੇ ਵੇਖੈ ਵਿਗਸੈ ਆਪੇ ਜਿਤੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਤਿਤੁ ਲਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: har aape vekhai vigasai aape jit bhaavai tith laae |
lines.english: The Lord Himself beholds, and He Himself rejoices. As He wills, so does He enjoin us.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਆਪੇ ਠਾਕੁਰੁ ਸੇਵਕੁ ਭਗਤੁ ਹਰਿ ਆਪੇ ਕਰੇ ਕਰਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: har aape tthaakur sevak bhagat har aape kare karaae |
lines.english: The Lord Himself is the Master, the servant and the devotee; the Lord Himself is the Cause of causes.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਸਚਾ ਸਾਹਿਬੁ ਸਚੁ ਤਪਾਵਸੁ ਕਰਿ ਵੇਖੈ ਚਲਤ ਸਬਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: har sachaa saahib sach tapaavas kar vekhai chalat sabaae |
lines.english: The Lord is the True Master; True is His justice. He arranges and beholds all His plays.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਇਕਨਾ ਮਾਰਗਿ ਪਾਏ ਆਪੇ ਹਰਿ ਇਕਨਾ ਉਝੜਿ ਪਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: har ikanaa maarag paae aape har ikanaa ujharr paae |
lines.english: The Lord places some on the Path, and the Lord leads others into the wilderness.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਾਦਿ ਕਹੈ ਜਨੁ ਨਾਨਕੁ ਹਰਿ ਸਚੇ ਕੇ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਏ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: gur parasaad kahai jan naanak har sache ke gun gaae |5|
lines.english: By Guru's Grace, servant Nanak speaks and sings the Glorious Praises of the True Lord. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਘਰਿ ਘਰਿ ਹੰਢੈ ਮੰਗਦਾ ਧਿਗੁ ਜੀਵਣੁ ਧਿਗੁ ਵੇਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: je ghar ghar handtai mangadaa dhig jeevan dhig ves |
lines.english: Cursed is the life, and cursed are the clothes, of one who wanders around, begging from door to door.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Salok, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਰਵੇਸੀ ਕੋ ਜਾਣਸੀ ਵਿਰਲਾ ਕੋ ਦਰਵੇਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: daravesee ko jaanasee viralaa ko daraves |
lines.english: How rare is the dervish, the Saintly renunciate, who understands renunciation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸ ਕੇ ਚਰਨ ਪਖਾਲੀਅਹਿ ਨਾਨਕ ਹਉ ਬਲਿਹਾਰੈ ਜਾਉ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: tis ke charan pakhaaleeeh naanak hau balihaarai jaau |1|
lines.english: then Nanak washes his feet, and is a sacrifice to him. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਆਸਾ ਅੰਦੇਸਾ ਤਜਿ ਰਹੈ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਭਿਖਿਆ ਨਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: je aasaa andesaa taj rahai guramukh bhikhiaa naau |
lines.english: But, if he abandons hope and anxiety, and as Gurmukh receives the Name as his charity,

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਤਰਵਰੁ ਏਕੁ ਫਲੁ ਦੁਇ ਪੰਖੇਰੂ ਆਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: naanak taravar ek fal due pankheroo aaeh |
lines.english: O Nanak, the tree has one fruit, but two birds are perched upon it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਵਤ ਜਾਤ ਨ ਦੀਸਹੀ ਨਾ ਪਰ ਪੰਖੀ ਤਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: aavat jaat na deesahee naa par pankhee taeh |
lines.english: They are not seen coming or going; these birds have no wings.

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਰਸਿ ਫਲਿ ਰਾਤੇ ਨਾਨਕਾ ਕਰਮਿ ਸਚਾ ਨੀਸਾਣੁ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: har ras fal raate naanakaa karam sachaa neesaan |2|
lines.english: Imbued with the subtle essence of the fruit of the Lord's Name, O Nanak, the soul bears the True Insignia of God's Grace. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਹੁ ਰੰਗੀ ਰਸ ਭੋਗਿਆ ਸਬਦਿ ਰਹੈ ਨਿਰਬਾਣੁ ॥
lines.translit: bahu rangee ras bhogiaa sabad rahai nirabaan |
lines.english: One enjoys so many pleasures, while the other, through the Word of the Shabad, remains in Nirvaanaa.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਿ ਪਕਾਵੈ ਆਪਿ ਭਾਂਡੇ ਦੇਇ ਪਰੋਸੈ ਆਪੇ ਹੀ ਬਹਿ ਖਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: aap pakaavai aap bhaandde dee parosai aape hee beh khaavai |
lines.english: He Himself cooks it, He Himself puts the food in the dishes, and He Himself sits down to eat.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਧਰਤੀ ਆਪੇ ਹੈ ਰਾਹਕੁ ਆਪਿ ਜੰਮਾਇ ਪੀਸਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: aape dharatee aape hai raahak aap jamaae peesaavai |
lines.english: He Himself is the field, and He Himself is the farmer. He Himself grows and grinds the corn.
